danger_signals.visual_form: Enemies spawning from arena passages on four edges
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent threat; player must react to incoming attackers

danger_signals.visual_form: Multiple enemy types present simultaneously on screen
danger_signals.meaning: High-threat state; player is under sustained attack pressure

danger_signals.visual_form: Sadistic host character emerges as final arena opponent
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated danger; culmination of level threat

safety_signals.visual_form: All enemies defeated; exit doors appear on screen
Temporary power-ups and weapons visible on arena floor
Money piles and gift-wrapped prizes scattered in cleared arenas
safety_signals.meaning: Safe looting period; reward phase between danger phases

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue (primary player in arcade version documentation)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Visible on arena floor as controllable avatar

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Sadistic persona; verbal brand identity ('Total carnage! I love it!', 'Big money! Big prizes!')
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Appears as final boss enemy with distinct visual presentation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dominates arena as authority figure; represents game show control system

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types entering from four arena edges
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Spawn from directional passages; represent show's hostile obstacles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Announcer describes 'the most violent game show of all time' in futuristic 1999 setting
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game world establishes dystopian television game show context; host verbally frames stakes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single-screen arenas with enemies entering in waves; money and prizes visible as environmental scatter
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players shoot incoming waves, collect items, navigate to exits; cooperative tension between players for cash/prize rewards

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Exits appear on screen; map displays overall level layout and boss location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players choose directional path forward; strategic decision point before next arena

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Host character becomes visible as final opponent; escalated visual threat density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Climactic encounter; host's verbal interjections punctuate combat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unlock triggered by collecting keys; distinct bonus arena aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High-reward collection opportunity; 'hundreds of blue bikini-clad babes' as prizes; represents show's materialistic excess

environmental_storytelling.example: Materialistic consumer goods and objectified rewards scattered throughout arenas communicate dystopian game show's base values—consumerism and exploitation
Four-directional entry points for enemy waves suggest controlled, staged arena design; reinforces game-show-within-a-game framing

Catchphrases ('Total carnage! I love it!', 'Big money! Big prizes!', 'I'd buy that for a dollar!') establish sadistic authority figure as narrator and antagonist; borrow dystopian tone from RoboCop/The Running Man source films
Map showing arena layout and boss location after first arena completion communicates structured, predetermined show progression; removes player agency from route choice
Players cannot return to previously cleared arenas; enforces forward momentum and prevents escape routes—communicates inescapable game show trap

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Enter arena in organized waves from four directional passages; escalate in density and complexity
Attack player character; require sustained shooting to eliminate
Provides verbal interjections throughout gameplay; establishes presence as game show authority
Emerges as final arena opponent; represents culmination of show's threat structure
Shoot enemies while collecting resources; at level end, compete for cash/prize rewards despite cooperation
Remain stationary on arena floor after enemies cleared; signal safe collection window

notes: Super Smash T.V. communicates dystopian world-state primarily through thematic narrative (game show framing, host authority, materialistic rewards) rather than environmental visual variation. The core visual manifestation is the arena's steady-state layout with dynamic enemy wave entry and prize scatter. Danger signals are behavior-based (wave spawning, density increase) rather than environmental color/lighting shifts. The game's political situation—contestants trapped in violent game show for entertainment and profit—is established through premise narration and host vocal performance rather than visual world-state evolution. Faction identity (host vs. players vs. attackers) is communicated through role positioning and verbal branding rather than distinct visual markers. Limited source documentation constrains depth of manifestation layer analysis.
sources_used: tcrf - Super Smash T.V. (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
